headline: Amazon will launch its satellite internet in South Africa, seemingly beating Musk in his homeland
summary: Amazon said Wednesday that the technology company will launch its new satellite internet service Amazon Leo in South Africa in 2027, seemingly pushing ahead of Elon Musk's rival Starlink to win business in Africa's most advanced economy.  Amazon, which was founded by Jeff Bezos, said that it would partner with South African internet provider Herotel to launch a new service in the country of 62 million people.

content.title: Amazon will launch its satellite internet in South Africa, seemingly beating Musk in his homeland
content.summary: Amazon said Wednesday that the technology company will launch its new satellite internet service Amazon Leo in South Africa in 2027, seemingly pushing ahead of Elon Musk's rival Starlink to win business in Africa's most advanced economy.  Amazon, which was founded by Jeff Bezos, said that it would partner with South African internet provider Herotel to launch a new service in the country of 62 million people.

headline: Amazon to launch S.Africa satellite internet as Starlink awaits licence
summary: Amazon said Wednesday it will launch satellite internet services in South Africa, entering a market where rival Elon Musk's Starlink has struggled to secure regulatory approval.Under the deal, Herotel -- South Africa's largest fixed internet service provider -- will use Amazon Leo's technology to offer a new service called Evry, slated for launch commercially in 2027.

headline: Amazon Leo to bring satellite internet to South Africa in 2027
summary: Amazon's low-earth orbit satellite internet venture Amazon Leo has signed an agreement with South Africa's Herotel ‌to launch a new broadband service aimed at connecting underserved ‌rural communities, it said on Wednesday.  Under the agreement, Herotel, South Africa's largest fixed internet service provider, ​will use Amazon Leo's satellite technology to offer a new service called evry, which is expected to launch commercially in 2027 for residential customers.

content.title: Amazon Leo to bring satellite internet to South Africa in 2027
content.summary: Amazon's low-earth orbit satellite internet venture Amazon Leo has signed an agreement with South Africa's Herotel ‌to launch a new broadband service aimed at connecting underserved ‌rural communities, it said on Wednesday.  Under the agreement, Herotel, South Africa's largest fixed internet service provider, ​will use Amazon Leo's satellite technology to offer a new service called evry, which is expected to launch commercially in 2027 for residential customers.

headline: What IBM’s Weak Report Reveals About the Market
summary: IBM’s weak preliminary report isn’t a sign of AI slowing but a leading indicator of the second wave of AI. Traditional enterprises are shifting budgets from software to hardware, proving that AI adoption has spread from Big Tech to the wider economy.

content.title: What IBM’s Weak Report Reveals About the Market
content.summary: IBM’s weak preliminary report isn’t a sign of AI slowing but a leading indicator of the second wave of AI. Traditional enterprises are shifting budgets from software to hardware, proving that AI adoption has spread from Big Tech to the wider economy.

headline: Plot Twist: 12 States Could Stall the Paramount-Warner Bros. Discovery Mega-Merger
summary: A 12-state antitrust lawsuit threatens to delay Paramount Skydance's $110 billion merger with Warner Bros. Discovery, exposing both companies' weak balance sheets to costly penalty deadlines.
